events.description: Senator Murray introduced S. 716, a bill authorizing the issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters, read twice and referred to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
George Peters wrote a letter to Senator Murray (referenced in Murray's January 26 reply)
Senator Murray replied to George Peters advising he had prepared and was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of a patent in fee
Department of the Interior (Undersecretary Oscar Chapman) issued report on S. 716 recommending enactment if amended to allow sale to a Crow Indian under existing regulations
Senator O'Mahoney, Chairman of Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, wrote to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report and asking if amendments were agreeable
House Committee on Public Lands reported favorably on S. 716 without amendment and recommended the bill do pass; committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed as Report No. 508
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and had been reported favorably out of the House Committee on Public Lands; enclosed copy of House report
S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Telegram sent to George Peters, Box 34, Wyola, Montana, advising that S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of Interior to permit Secretary to sell the land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
George Peters indicated his desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters' allotment No. 2292 on Crow Indian Reservation to another Crow Indian under existing regulations, as authorized by S. 716
